Opening lines do not vary or stop the sc.hook_focus: Hook fix: prefer curiosity/interrupt/question opening patterns with a crisp contrast line. Avoid generic “first line” openers; ensure the first line clearly signals a specific promise + reason to keep watching.
Opening lines do not vary or stop the sc.hashtag-generator: Hashtag fix: keep hashtags tightly aligned with one clear niche promise. Prefer 10–18 highly related niche tags; avoid broad/mixed-topic tags that dilute the first-line hook.
Opening lines do not vary or stop the sc.title-generator: Title fix: use explicit curiosity gap or question/contradiction structure in the first part of the title. Make the title carry the same promise as the hook (no vague hype).

Content format is too scattered for the.hook_focus: Focus fix: pick one dominant content format/structure for the next 3–5 posts and repeat it consistently. Keep niche keywords stable; avoid mixing multiple angles inside a single package.
Content format is too scattered for the.hashtag-generator: Hashtag focus: output a more consistent, repeating hashtag set that matches your dominant format. Keep tags from drifting across unrelated topics.
Content format is too scattered for the.title-generator: Title focus: keep titles within one repeatable search intent pattern; avoid switching between unrelated formats in consecutive generations.

CTA is missing on most posts.hook_focus: CTA fix: include one explicit action once the proof/pacing is established. Keep CTA clear and concrete (comment/save/follow/link), and ensure it appears in both caption/script where relevant.
CTA is missing on most posts.hashtag-generator: Hashtag CTA support: add one branded/engagement-oriented tag (not salesy) that supports your chosen CTA goal.
CTA is missing on most posts.title-generator: Title CTA support: reinforce the intended engagement action in the wording without making it feel like hard sell.

Heavy CTA while the stated goal is reach.hook_focus: CTA placement fix: avoid sales-heavy language in the hook/opening line. Keep CTA soft and placed later (after proof) as save/follow/comment oriented engagement.
Heavy CTA while the stated goal is reach.hashtag-generator: Avoid hard-sell hashtags: prefer community/engagement/supportive tags over purchase/shopping urgency tags.
Heavy CTA while the stated goal is reach.title-generator: Avoid “buy now” style titles; keep titles focused on curiosity/reach rather than direct offers.

Educational / calm.hook_focus: Tone fix: keep a calm, guide-like voice. Prefer structured “how to / steps / tips” style hook openings.
Educational / calm.hashtag-generator: Tone fix: prefer niche “how-to” oriented tags and keep them instructional rather than promotional.
Educational / calm.title-generator: Tone fix: use educational structure (steps/how-to) with one curiosity gap in the opening part.

Conversational / friendly.hook_focus: Tone fix: use friendly, conversational framing. Choose question/curiosity openers and maintain clarity in the hook promise.
Conversational / friendly.hashtag-generator: Tone fix: choose relatable niche tags that match your audience language; avoid overly formal promotional tags.
Conversational / friendly.title-generator: Tone fix: keep titles simple and conversation-like, with one clear curiosity hook.

Direct response / salesy.hook_focus: Tone fix: still protect the hook: lead with a problem + promise + short proof tease; delay hard sell until after the first retention beat.
Direct response / salesy.hashtag-generator: Tone fix: keep salesy tone only in the later part; hashtags should remain niche + credibility oriented.
Direct response / salesy.title-generator: Tone fix: mix discovery with conversion wording—avoid forcing direct offer in the first clause.

growth.hook_focus: Optimization: prioritize retention-first hooks (curiosity/interrupt) and soft CTAs (save/follow/comment). Avoid sales-heavy patterns too early.
growth.hashtag-generator: Optimization: choose narrower niche tags that improve reach + saves (not urgency).
growth.title-generator: Optimization: titles should aim for discovery (what/how/why) rather than direct selling.

conversion.hook_focus: Optimization: include one conversion-oriented beat, but keep it aligned with retention. Use proof-led framing and end with a clear CTA after the hook promise.
conversion.hashtag-generator: Optimization: include a single engagement/CTA-support tag set; keep remaining tags niche and credible.
conversion.title-generator: Optimization: title should signal both relevance and the outcome (clear promise + light CTA framing).
